steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has at least one solution when the lines are not parallel — they either intersect (one solution) or are identical (infinitely many solutions). Test each equation to see whether it creates a parallel line, intersecting line, or the same line as $7x + 5y = 2$.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Ⅰ only': ignores whether Ⅱ also works. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Ⅱ only': ignores whether Ⅰ also works. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Ⅰ and Ⅱ': Equation Ⅰ: divide $10.5x + 7.5y = 3$ by $1.5$ to get $7x + 5y = 2$ — the same line, so infinitely many solutions. ✓ Equation Ⅱ: rewrite $10.5x - 7.5y = 3$ as $y = \frac{7}{5}x - \frac{2}{5}$ (slope $\frac{7}{5}$). The original has slope $-\frac{7}{5}$. Different slopes mean they intersect — one solution. ✓ Both allow at least one solution. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Neither Ⅰ nor Ⅱ': both equations produce systems with solutions, so this is wrong. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
